svg.text: interview entry point — wraps ONE service
errors as return values — get() ⇒ null = MISS
no checked exceptions on the hot path
the seam — swap impls behind this interface ✓
WAL append happens BEFORE the write lock — durability first
ONE lock guards store + evictionPolicy so they never diverge ✓
the class the interviewer reads first
in-memory list here (FileChannel + fsync in prod)
STRATEGY knob — swap LRU / LFU / FIFO
access-order map = O(1) LRU, no hand-rolled DLL
sweeps expired keys under the write lock
lazy expire on GET + active background sweep
THE MODEL — what the store holds
no version field — CAS is aspirational, not built
put ⇒ buffer · commit ⇒ flush · rollback ⇒ clear
staging buffer — commit replays each via put()
no version validation / isolation in this impl
ctor + getData() both defensive-copy the map
immutable snapshot ⇒ safe to restore later
point-in-time copy taken under the read lock

g.text: Append to the log BEFORE touching
write is simply lost, store stays
many readers OR one writer. get()
takes the read lock; put / delete /
evict take the write lock — so the
store & LRU order stay in sync.
to purge a key it found expired.

svg.text: ① KVStore facade → KVStoreService seam → ② InMemoryKVStore (map + WAL + lock + scheduler) → ③ collaborators: WAL, EvictionPolicy Strategy, TTL sweeper → ④ model (KeyValue / Transaction / Snapshot)

g.text: Patterns: Facade · Strategy (eviction) · WAL · ReadWriteLock · write-buffer txn
In-memory KV store — WAL durability, TTL, LRU eviction, snapshots — LLD
